compressed_prompt: write song lyrics on socio-economic issue, all lowercase, no commas
response:
the alarm clock screams
another day of the same machine
i punch the clock
you count the stock
we’re all just cogs and barely seen

the rent climbs high
a heavy sigh
the dream is getting thin
while on the screen
a shiny scene
of where we’ll never win

we’re running on a wheel of never enough
trading all our hours for the basic stuff
the gap is growing wide a canyon in the sun
the many work for less the race is never won

the factory gate
now holds a weight
of silence and of rust
the profit soars
behind closed doors
in palaces of trust

they sell the lie
just scrape the sky
the ladder’s made of glass
the bottom rung
holds everyone
waiting for a pass

we’re running on a wheel of never enough
trading all our hours for the basic stuff
the gap is growing wide a canyon in the sun
the many work for less the race is never won

a single bill
can break the will
a fever takes the child
the safety net
is frayed and wet
the gentle voice turns wild

but in the street
beneath the heat
a murmur starts to grow
a common beat
a bitter sweet
the only truth we know

we’ll break the wheel and build a world of enough
share the heavy burden when the road is rough
the gap will close at last in a new morning sun
the many stand as one the race is finally won
